Affliction Haste II
QuoteReply
# Nov 15 2002 at 5:21 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591764517843
14 posts
Anyone know if this spell-haste would affect poison cast times?
▲ Page top
RE: Affliction Haste II
QuoteReply
# Nov 27 2002 at 3:41 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760137454
99 posts
Gives reduced Cast time on Poison and Disease Dots as well as on Root type spells.
Has reduced my Root Casting time to 1.4 from 2.0!
▲ Page top
RE: Affliction Haste II
QuoteReply
# Feb 13 2003 at 8:06 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592341837046
View User Forum
Scholar
582 posts
As of last week, this no longer helps with non-damaging spells (not Slows, Roots, or other debuffs unless they have a DoT component).

affliction haste 2
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2002 at 4:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253526621
6 posts

ok this is my First hand knowledge so far My 28 Necro has Ring of the Goblin Lords affliction Haste 1 it lowered my cast times on dots to 0.0

i dinged 29 and it nerfed so i am assuming affliction haste 2 items will do the same but over 29 to say? 40? then you would need an affliction haste 3 items it eleminates casting time on affliction spells such as Dots.

Wrathos 16SK
Shadowthorne Darkbane 29 Necro
▲ Page top
RE: affliction haste 2
QuoteReply
# Jun 07 2002 at 4:49 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591755048933
Scholar
127 posts
There was a major fudge up on some of the haste items in yesterdays patch. I had a friend who was casting Clarity with a 0.0 cast time before the emergency patch.

The stated effect is a percentage reduction in cast time, so the way it is working now is the way, at least according to VI, that it *should* work. For the 8 or so hours yesterday that that bug was active, things were kinda interesting for some folks, methinks.
▲ Page top

Lotta hp
QuoteReply
# May 29 2002 at 1:08 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759076529
95 posts
Dropped her tonight with myself 52sk and ranger who was 46. Rangers had to snare 4 times and i envoked fear atleast 10 it seemed. Long fight but for the most part we didnt get scratched. Sigra did not turn when low on ife though and commenced to pulping me like an orange. I was oom so we ghetto kited her all over the zone. How we didnt get a single add I have no clue. All in all a great night. My friend kept the SCFG to use since he is working on his epic. Good luck to all this is a great item.
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Dec 08 2001 at 4:35 AM Rating: Decent

21 posts
This belt rocks! Just killed Sigra with 48 ranger, 51 cleric and me (47 rogue). Was very close until our warrior friend showed up to help deal the final blows. She was yellow to me and tuff as heck, but dropped the belt. I was happy :)

▲ Page top
Rogue Belt
QuoteReply
# Oct 16 2001 at 3:10 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Best Rogue Belt under 8k i think.
Dex helps alot to hit mobs. To much str is useless when you have to many misses cause you lack dex.
▲ Page top
RE: Rogue Belt
QuoteReply
# May 13 2002 at 7:13 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591772168893
4 posts
Actually, DEX does not have anything to do with to-hit %. Surprisingly enough, it is all STR related. DEX does, however, have to do with weapon procs and rogue skills. Not sure if backstabs are affected somehow or not, or if it is only non-combat types of skills (pick-pocket, pick-lock, etc.).
▲ Page top
RE: Rogue Belt
QuoteReply
# May 14 2002 at 3:59 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591778236936
23 posts
Dex also helps you improve your melee combat skills. If you consider the fact that it will help you learn your combat skills faster, then you could argue that it helps get you to a skill level where you may hit more often.

But when it comes down to it, feel free to correct me if I'm wrong, the actual calculation to determine whether or not you will land a hit depends on your attack number. Something like this:

+ level
+ offensive skill
+ weapon skill (rogue = piercing)
+ strength
- mob lvl
- mob defensive skills
- mob AC
----------
= Attack

(I know there are a lot of percentages involved but I wanted to keep it basic)

Have you ever noticed when you get a buff that adds 20-25 str you get a lot bigger number next to "Attack" on your stats/inventory screen? It's right under your HP and AC.

If you want to verify what elements help in your attack or even what weapon you should be using, change your inventory, armor, or weapons around and see if the attack number goes up or down. For melee classes, WAR SK PAL RNG ROG MNK (BST BRD depending on skills), I would get that number as high as possible, unless you are tanking then you need to keep up your AC. But again some would argue that attack is more important than AC because you kill mobs faster with a higher attack number.

-Quelek
30th Paladin
Quellious
▲ Page top
RE: Rogue Belt
QuoteReply
# Jun 04 2003 at 3:15 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1710720965640
175 posts
ATK has nothing to do with the mobs stats. It does change when you target a mob.

Whether you actually hit them or not does take the mobs stats into account though.
▲ Page top
RE: Rogue Belt
QuoteReply
# Jun 07 2002 at 4:43 PM Rating: Default
Tasen
22 posts
You dont get any atk bunuses based on your level, I have a 46 Ranger with a 0 skill in 2h blunt, and I was running around CB missing like crazy, well my atk was still 400 or so from srt and offense skill, but I was still missing more often than I was hitting

wrong.

▲ Page top
Drop Info
QuoteReply
# Oct 15 2001 at 1:27 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591761453921
Scholar
45 posts
This item drops off of Sigra in Trak's Teeth. Sigra is a giant sabertooth that can spawn at several levels, but is often level 50. Sigra is a straight melee mob with no special attacks.

Sigra spawns off the Hunter cycle. Kill enough Froglok Hunters and she will eventually show up.
▲ Page top
Sirga
QuoteReply
# Sep 04 2001 at 11:55 PM Rating: Decent

confused
QuoteReply
# Jul 27 2001 at 3:38 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591751447139
3 posts
Don't know whether to wear this or my fbss
I'm a lvl 27 bard with a sonachie's partisan and a CSS, don't know if i want the haste or the dex
▲ Page top
RE: confused
QuoteReply
# Dec 22 2002 at 5:21 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763705560
View User Forum
105 posts
All depends on your dex without it and if you are meleeing. I am a swarm kite bard so I would wear these over the FBSS for the dex. My dex is currently 123 while kiting and I miss a lot of notes. Kinda sucks so I'm on the lookout for dex gear. Trying to get kiting dex over 150, without sacrificing my other important stats like cha and int.

I'd say switch them out when you want to melee, go FBSS, if you want to play support or kite, then the dex is very useful if you are dex challenged like me.

My bardic 2cp

Sinfonian, bard of the 51 song on my way to baron fast.
